questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. The reason Radha was late [a]/ to the office is because she [b]/ was distributing books among the poor children [c]/ No error [d]
options: to the office is because she
was distributing books among the poor children
questions.explanation: 2nd Segment is erroneous. To rectify the sentence change ‘because’ with ‘that’. Rule: The reason is not followed by because, because of, due to, owing to. Because “The reason” is itself a cause. Hence the correct sentence is: The reason Radha was late to the office is that she was distributing books among the poor children.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Back out
questions.explanation: The correct answer is ‘to withdraw’. Back out means to decide that you are no longer going to take part in something that has been agreed.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Ravenous
questions.explanation: The correct answer is ‘Starved’. Ravenous: extremely hungry; devouring or craving food in great quantities Starved: to suffer or die because you do not have enough food to eat; to make somebody/something suffer or die in this way. Content: happy or satisfied with what you have or do. Satiated: to completely satisfy a desire or need.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. SURREPTITIOUS
questions.explanation: The correct answer is ‘Secret’. Surreptitious: marked by quiet and caution and secrecy; taking pains to avoid being observed. Hesitation: a certain degree of unwillingness. Secret: not open or public; kept private or not revealed. Impious: lacking due respect or dutifulness. Artless: simple and natural; without cunning or deceit.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into three segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. If not then choose no error option. Ritika decided to get up early/ to wear a nice dress / and visit her aunt.
options: Ritika decided to get up early
questions.explanation: Third segment is erroneous. To visit someone means to go see a person. The correct sentence is: Ritika decided to get up early to wear a nice dress and to visit her aunt.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. The student asked me if / I knew that Kalidas was the greater / than any other poet / No error.
options: I knew that Kalidas was the greater
questions.explanation: Second segment is erroneous. We use comparative degree when we compare between two person or thing. But if comparison is between one person to the all person then we use superlative degree. Hence the correct sentence is: The student asked me if I knew that Kalidas was the greatest than any other poet.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. Words written on the tomb of a person.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Epitaph. Epitaph: Words written on the tomb of a person. Epigram: a short poem or phrase that expresses an idea in a clever or amusing wayEpicure: a person who takes particular pleasure in fine food and drink.Epitaph: words that are written or said about a dead person, especially words written on a stone where he/she is buried.

questions.question: Select the correct passive voice of the given sentence. I will write an essay
options: An essay will have been written by me.
An essay will be written by me.
An essay has been written by me.
An essay had been written by me.
questions.explanation: Option b is the correct answer. Rule of Active to passive: Subject + will + v1 + Obj --- Obj + will be + v3 + by + sub. Hence the correct passive voice is: An essay will be written by me.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in Direct/Indirect speech. Neha said, “Need I write a letter?”
options: Neha asked if she have to write a letter.
Neha asked if she had been writing a letter.
Neha asked of writing a letter.
Neha asked if she had to write a letter.
questions.explanation: Option d is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. It is mainly due to Peter’s lethargy that the plan fell _________________.
questions.explanation: Option b is the correct answer. Fall through: to fail or not happen, Fall off: become detached and drop to the ground. Fall in: take one's place in a line or formation. Fall within: to belong to a particular area of activity.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. Lacking interest or excitement
questions.explanation: Option b is the correct answer. Mundane: ordinary; not interesting or exciting.Despot: a ruler with great power, especially one who uses it in a cruel way Miserly: thrifty. Hostile: having very strong feelings against somebody/something.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in active voice. He is given proper guidance by his boss.
options: He gives proper guidance to his boss.
His boss gives him proper guidance.
His boss is giving him proper guidance.
His boss has been giving him proper guidance.
questions.explanation: Option b is the correct answer. Rule: Active to Passive: Sub + v1 + s/es + obj ----- Obj + is/am/are + v3 + by + sub.Hence the correct active form of the given sentence is : His boss gives him proper guidance.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. At one’s wits end
questions.explanation: Option b is the correct answer. At one’s wits end means Completely puzzled and perplexed, not knowing what to do.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. Thus, shared laughter is one of the most effective tools in maintaining healthy relationships. B. Emotional sharing builds strong and lasting bonds. C. It also helps in healing resentments and hurt. D. But sharing laughter brings joy and freshness to relationships.
questions.explanation: Option c is the correct answer. In the given sentence we can start only with option b because this sentence start with the title ‘emotional sharing’. The correct arrangement is: Emotional sharing builds strong and lasting bonds. But sharing laughter brings joy and freshness to relationships. It also helps in healing resentments and hurt. Thus, shared laughter is one of the most effective tools in maintaining healthy relationships.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Obdurate
questions.explanation: The correct antonym is Amenable. Obdurate: refusing to change your mind to do what people want you to do. Amenable: happy to accept something; willing to be influenced by somebody/something.Rigid: not able to or not wanting to change or be changed.Callous: not caring about the feelings or suffering of other people.Secure: free from worry or doubt; confident

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: Option d is the correct answer. The correct spelling is Temperate(used about a climate) not very hot and not very cold. Other three are correctly spelt.

questions.question: The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them may contain a grammatical error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. If you don’t find any error, mark ‘No error’ as your answer. They had hardly completed / half of the work / than the boss called them.
questions.explanation: 3rd part contains error. Hardly/Scarcely is always followed by ‘when’. Hence to make the sentence correct we will replace when with than. The correct sentence is: They had hardly completed half of the work when the boss called them.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. If it would rain [a]/ they will[b]/ not come. [c]/ No error [d]
questions.explanation: 1st part is erroneous. Would will not come because of ‘If’ and rains will come instead of rain. The correct sentence is: If it rains they will not come.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. IMBROGLIO
questions.explanation: Option c is the correct answer. Imbroglio: a situation that is complicated, confusing or embarrassing, especially a political or public one.Composure: the state of being calm and having your feelings under control. Misery: great unhappiness or sufferingCensure: to tell somebody, in a strong and formal way, that he/she has done something wrong.Dilemma: a situation in which you have to make a difficult choice between two or more things.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. ______ did she open her lunch box than everyone gathered around her.
questions.explanation: Option c suits the best.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. The quest for a 1. ____________ life engrosses every human being on this earth. Every man tends to define a happy life in a 2. _______________ individualistic fashion. 3. ___________________ have attempted to define a happy life in various terms. Hedonists have a 4. ________________ notion that happiness lies in the 5. _________________ of physical appetites. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no.1.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is happy.The whole paragraph is about happiness hence the appropriate word is happy.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. The quest for a 1. ____________ life engrosses every human being on this earth. Every man tends to define a happy life in a 2. _______________ individualistic fashion. 3. ___________________ have attempted to define a happy life in various terms. Hedonists have a 4. ________________ notion that happiness lies in the 5. _________________ of physical appetites. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no.2.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate word to fill in the blank is ‘distinctly’. Which means particularly. Identical, similar and serious don’t make sense here because of happiness. Special suggestion to solve cloze test: Read the whole paragraph first then answer it.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. The quest for a 1. ____________ life engrosses every human being on this earth. Every man tends to define a happy life in a 2. _______________ individualistic fashion. 3. ___________________ have attempted to define a happy life in various terms. Hedonists have a 4. ________________ notion that happiness lies in the 5. _________________ of physical appetites. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no.3.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate word to fill in the blank is ‘Thinkers’. Professor, Researchers and Scientist makes any conclusion based on data, fact, analysis, Observations, theory etc. Abstract things like happiness, sadness…. Etc. is Thinkers or Philosophers things.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. The quest for a 1. ____________ life engrosses every human being on this earth. Every man tends to define a happy life in a 2. _______________ individualistic fashion. 3. ___________________ have attempted to define a happy life in various terms. Hedonists have a 4. ________________ notion that happiness lies in the 5. _________________ of physical appetites. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no.4.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate word to fill in the blank is “simple”.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. The quest for a 1. ____________ life engrosses every human being on this earth. Every man tends to define a happy life in a 2. _______________ individualistic fashion. 3. ___________________ have attempted to define a happy life in various terms. Hedonists have a 4. ________________ notion that happiness lies in the 5. _________________ of physical appetites. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no.5.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate word is gratification. For appetites only gratification makes sense. Appetites: a strong desire for something, especially food.Gratification: pleasure, especially when gained from the satisfaction of a desire.
